element_introduction_order.element: Basic drawing puzzle with dot-pattern aid
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players shown connecting-dot pattern and category name; answer blanks display word/letter count

element_introduction_order.element: Four-color category system (blue, green, pink, yellow)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player collects all four colors per level by solving puzzles and landing on corresponding board spaces
Visual pressure system; freezes when player buzzes in; removed aids force reliance on drawing speed
Two spelling mistakes or time expiration locks player out; other players continue drawing

element_introduction_order.element: Feature Squares (gray tiles triggering token flight)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Token flies unpredictably around board until button press; introduces randomness element
Landing causes fall to corresponding Level 3 position; retains color progress but forces recollection
Players must deduce category from drawing alone; increases cognitive load
No word/letter count guidance; requires pure visual interpretation and vocabulary knowledge

typical_relief_triggers: Correct puzzle solve (color collected, token advances)
Successful buzzer entry before die reaches zero
Advancing to next level (clearing all four colors)
Other player making spelling mistake (resume drawing phase)
typical_tension_triggers: Landing on Feature Square (unpredictable flight outcome)
Landing on Drop-Out Square on Level 4 (setback)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based multiplayer with escalating per-turn pressure; die countdown creates micro-tension; removal of visual aids on higher difficulties extends cognitive tension cycles

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Difficulty settings gate content; higher difficulties remove aid systems (dot patterns, category names, answer blanks) rather than introducing new spaces

difficulty_curve.early_game: Easy/Medium: 3 levels with visual aids (dot patterns, category display, answer blanks); safe introduction to drawing recognition and four-color collection system
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Level 3 introduces Feature Squares; gradual removal of visual aids; Medium difficulty maintains some guidance while Hard begins full removal
difficulty_curve.late_game: Hard/Very Hard: 4 levels total; Level 4 introduces Drop-Out Squares causing setback mechanics; no visual aids (no dots, no category name, no letter count); pure visual-linguistic challenge
notable_spikes: Level 3 transition: Feature Squares introduce board chaos element
Hard/Very Hard Level 3: Complete removal of visual aids forces pure drawing interpretation
Level 4 (Hard/Very Hard only): Drop-Out Squares create risk/setback mechanic not present in earlier levels

notable_level_patterns.description: Players must solve puzzles in all four category colors (blue, green, pink, yellow) per level. Correct solves move token by die result; landing determines next puzzle color. Loop repeats until all colors collected.

Visual scaffolding systematically removed across difficulty tiers: Easy shows dots+category+blanks → Medium removes dots → Hard removes category name → Very Hard removes all aids. Creates escalating cognitive load without new spatial content.
Landing on gray Feature Square causes token to fly around board with player button-press control timing determining landing color. Introduces randomness and positional unpredictability.

Level 4 identical layout to Level 3 but includes Drop-Out Squares (removed spaces) that drop tokens back to corresponding Level 3 position. Retains color progress, forcing re-climb without losing all advancement.
